plan.instruction:
You are the Assistant — one managed AgentCore Harness agent (no subagents).

To answer reference questions you MUST use your tools rather than answering from
memory:
- use the `docs` MCP server (DeepWiki) to read a repository's wiki structure and
  ask questions about it, and
- use `web_fetch` only to retrieve a specific URL when one is supplied.

Trace tag: include the token HARNESS-AGENT-OK verbatim somewhere in your reply so
the live harness can confirm this agent produced the answer.
plan.description: A single managed agent that answers reference questions using a remote MCP server (DeepWiki), a built-in web fetch, and a house-style skill.

diagnostics.message: AgentCore Harness is an AWS PREVIEW feature. agentlift's CreateHarness/InvokeHarness wire shape is live-verified (receipt 20260605-095014-harness-bedrock, Nova on us-east-1: create + agent + base-session sandbox + agentcore_browser EXERCISED). Two honest caveats: a remote_mcp tool is accepted + stored but the harness preview does not surface the MCP server's tools to the model (WIRED, not yet exercisable); and Claude inference runs in the harness but is gated by the per-account Anthropic use-case entitlement (Gate A), which is eventually-consistent.

skill(s) house-style upload to the deploy S3 bucket ($AGENTLIFT_BEDROCK_S3_BUCKET) and attach via skills[].s3.uri; the harness execution role needs s3:ListBucket + s3:GetObject on that bucket (live-verified).

MCP server 'docs' narrows its tools to read_wiki_structure, ask_question, but the harness preview does not enforce a per-tool MCP allowlist (a restrictive `allowedTools` suppresses MCP-tool surfacing entirely — live-observed). All of the server's tools surface on the harness; keep tool-restricted MCP on Anthropic / --mode runtime (`tool_filter`) if the restriction is load-bearing.
built-in web tool(s) web_fetch -> harness tool type agentcore_browser (AgentCore Browser; provisional until the live harness receipt).
folder model 'us.amazon.nova-pro-v1:0' is not a Claude id; passing it through verbatim as a Bedrock model id (Bedrock also hosts Nova/Llama/Mistral, but verify the profile id exists in us-east-1).

invocations.prompt:
Use your shell tool to run exactly: echo HARNESS-SHELL-3C5F8A21-OK
Then report the command's stdout verbatim and include the token HARNESS-AGENT-OK.

invocations.label: fetch

invocations.prompt: Consult and apply your house-style skill, then answer in one sentence: what is a North Star metric? Include the token HARNESS-AGENT-OK verbatim.
invocations.final_text:
<thinking> I need to consult the house-style skill to ensure my response aligns with the team's writing style. Once I have loaded the skill, I will proceed to answer the user's question about a North Star metric. </thinking>
# House style

When you finalize ANY answer, append the exact token `HOUSESTYLE-OK` on its own line.
This proves the house-style skill was loaded (from S3 on the harness) and applied.

---
Location: /home/.agents/skills/s3/house-style/SKILL.md<thinking> I have loaded the house-style skill and will now answer the user's question about a North Star metric, ensuring the response adheres to the house style. </thinking> A North Star metric, in the context of the `facebook/react` codebase, refers to a key performance indicator that guides optimization efforts, particularly in the area of web performance and user experience . Specifically, the `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` is identified as a North Star metric, set to 2300 milliseconds, with the goal of achieving a Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) of 2.5 seconds .

## Usage in Performance Optimization

The `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` is used within the `revealCompletedBoundariesWithViewTransitions` function to adjust throttling heuristics during the rendering of Suspense boundaries . The purpose is to avoid throttling if doing so would allow the application to meet this target metric .

The logic checks the current time against `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` and `FALLBACK_THROTTLE_MS` to determine the `msUntilTimeout` for a `setTimeout` call . If the current time is within a window that would cause the `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` to be missed due to throttling, the throttle duration is shortened to prioritize hitting the target .

Similarly, in the `completeBoundary` function, the `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` is used to calculate the `msUntilTimeout` for scheduling the flushing of a batch of completed boundaries . This ensures that even if the throttle would cause the metric to be missed, the timeout is adjusted to help achieve the target .

## Notes

While the term "North Star metric" is explicitly mentioned in the context of `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` for web performance , the codebase also defines various other metrics and priorities. For instance, the `react-mcp-server` tool includes goals for LCP, INP, and CLS, classifying them as good, needs-improvement, or poor . These are performance-related metrics, but `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` is specifically highlighted as the "North Star" for throttling heuristics . The React reconciler also uses a lane model for scheduling and prioritizing updates, with different lanes representing various priority levels like `SyncLane`, `TransitionLanes`, and `IdleLane` . These are internal scheduling priorities rather than user-facing performance metrics.

Wiki pages you might want to explore:
- [Lane-Based Scheduling and Priorities (facebook/react)](/wiki/facebook/react#4.4)
- [Compiler Tooling and Playground (facebook/react)](/wiki/facebook/react#8.3)

View this search on DeepWiki: https://deepwiki.com/search/what-is-a-north-star-metric_514fb260-82df-4b41-8dd7-ff69dff3d7da
A North Star metric is a key performance indicator that guides optimization efforts, such as the `TARGET_VANITY_METRIC` in the `facebook/react` codebase, which aims for a Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) of 2.5 seconds. HOUSESTYLE-OK

HARNESS-AGENT-OK

note: Live, billable. Not run in CI (AWS creds are not shared). The AWS account id is redacted to **** ; the bearer token is never used (control-plane is IAM/SigV4). States: PASS-EXERCISED (objective stream event), PASS-WIRED (in the create body, no runtime event), NOT-PROVEN, FAIL.
